tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine, low total CO₂, and marked proteinuria, suggesting impaired renal function with possible metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals glucosuria, ketonuria, acidic pH, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, raising suspicion for diabetic ketoacidosis or another metabolic disturbance, despite a low HbA1c. Immediate clinical evaluation for acute or chronic kidney disease and metabolic derangements is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus

text.div: Provider Organization: Azienda Sanitaria Locale n° 7 Musei
